versions.prompt: Very dark abstract texture for a card background. Subtle granite and pine bark texture blended together. Dark earthy tones — deep grey-brown (#15130F) fading to near-black (#0A0908). A barely visible hint of forest green in the shadows. Must be very dark (90%+ dark) so white text is readable on top. Feels like Sierra Nevada granite and old-growth forest. Organic, natural, weathered — not digital or clean.
versions.analysis: Dark bark/granite texture is convincingly Sierra Nevada. Very dark and uniform — excellent for text overlay. The organic, weathered feel is strong. Distinct from CO and UT textures.
versions.iterate_notes: Could use a hint more of the forest green in the shadows as specified. Currently reads very monochrome.

versions.prompt: Sierra granite with dark green lichen patches, grey-brown stone texture.
versions.analysis: Dark granite with green lichen patches. Very Sierra Nevada — the lichen adds the forest green that v0 was missing.
versions.iterate_notes: Try old-growth redwood bark with deep furrows for a more distinctly California texture.

versions.prompt: Old-growth redwood bark with deep furrows, dark red-brown texture, massive tree surface.
versions.analysis: Beautiful red-brown bark texture with deep vertical furrows. Distinctly California — you can feel the scale of the tree.
versions.iterate_notes: Try forest floor pine needles and pine cones — Tahoe forest floor for an organic ground-level texture.

versions.prompt: Forest floor pine needles and pine cones, dark organic texture, Tahoe forest floor.
versions.analysis: Very organic Tahoe forest floor. Pine needles create a natural pattern with warm brown tones. Pine cones add visual interest.
versions.iterate_notes: Try wet dark granite with water film — river stone look with subtle mica sparkle.

versions.prompt: Wet dark granite with water film, smooth river stone surface, subtle mica sparkle.
versions.analysis: Smooth river stone with mica sparkle and a thin water film. Dark and reflective — very refined Sierra feel.
versions.iterate_notes: Could combine the wet granite with subtle lichen from v1 for added character. Or try a frost-on-granite variant for winter feel.
